description: Canonical registry of every WorldMonitor-derived data subset azimuth may surface. The per-source license guardrail (scripts/check_sources.py) validates every entry before any subset is surfaced as L1. A subset that is not in this registry, or whose entry fails the guardrail, MUST NOT be surfaced. See docs/source-guardrail.md. Each surfaced source carries a `theme` that maps it to one L2 brief (see `themes` below); excluded sources carry a `surfaced_reason` explaining why they stay surfaced:false.

content_class_policy.note: denied classes are the azimuth editorial exclusions (spec.md L3 rule set). prediction-market raw odds are allowed as L1 DATA but carry a synthesis caution forbidding investment framing in L2.

prediction-markets.title: Prediction Markets Weekly
prediction-markets.hold_reason: L1 ingest is ACTIVE (channel passes the guardrail). The L2 brief is held one cycle: the live WorldMonitor prediction feed currently surfaces a single, politically-charged market, which cannot be synthesised into a neutral public brief without centering a political figure. Brief lands once the feed carries a broader, non-single-political market set. Michael decision: confirm whether prediction-markets should be briefed at all under the editorial line.

sources.surfaced_reason: EXCLUDED: content_class political-opinion + risk_flags political-opinion/safety-prediction are on the editorial deny-list, and the upstream license is unknown (not in the allowlist). Fails the source-guardrail on two independent grounds; may never be surfaceable under the current editorial line.

EXCLUDED: content_class safety-prediction is on the editorial deny-list and the upstream license is unknown. Maritime/AIS would be the 'Maritime' theme but cannot pass the guardrail without a clean license AND a non-safety re-classification.

EXCLUDED: content_class safety-prediction + risk_flags safety-prediction/political-opinion on the editorial deny-list, license unknown. Air-traffic/ADS-B would be the 'Air-traffic' theme but is doubly editorial-excluded; not surfaceable under the current editorial line.
